description: List FISH -related quantities. By default all FISH symbols are listed. Alternatively a specific FISH symbol s can be listed.

keywords.name: arrays
keywords.description: List all FISH arrays that have been created.

keywords.name: basic
keywords.description: A basic list of all global FISH symbols.

keywords.name: callbacks
keywords.description: List the FISH functions that have been added to be executed as callbacks (i.e., with the fish callback command).

keywords.name: callback-list
keywords.description: List the callback events that have been registered. For instance, the solve_complete event is a registered callback event. [CS: is the base list of events shown anywhere??]

keywords.name: code
keywords.description: List the FISH pseudo-code generated after parsing FISH syntax. If a specific symbol s is provided, only the code for that specific function is listed. Otherwise the pseudo-code for all functions will be listed.

keywords.name: contents
keywords.description: For FISH values of an aggregate type (arrays, matrices, structures, maps, etc.) this will list the contents of the type in a structured way.

keywords.name: intrinsics
keywords.description: List the complete set of FISH intrinsics, both built-in and custom intrinsics created with the C++ Plugin option. If the string sfrag is given, only those intrinsics that contain sfrag in the name will be listed.

keywords.name: memory-items
keywords.description: List of all memory objects allocated. See the FISH function memory.create .

keywords.name: structures
keywords.description: List defined FISH structures, including their member names and values.

keywords.name: s
keywords.description: List the value of the symbol named s . If the symbol is a variable, the current value is listed. If the symbol is a function, the return value is listed and the function is executed. Remember to use the @ character at the start of the symbol name (e.g., @s ).

keywords.name: symbols
keywords.description: List all FISH symbols.

keywords.description: List all FISH arrays that have been created.
